# Narrative Reliefs From the SW and NW Corner Pavilions of Angkor Wat  ...    This study examines the religious and social meaning of the reliefs from the western corner pavilions of the temple of Angkor Wat (12th century AD.). Northern arm, West wall (above the window) The famous episode of the ordeal of Sita is represented here. The surface of the relief has been degraded by water infiltration to the point that the figure of Sita has completely disappeared. Sita was subjected to ordeal by fire soon after she was freed, in order to prove her purity. All that can now be seen, over several registers, is a group of moneys humorously represented, the pyre, and traces of the figures of Rama, Lakshmana, Sugriva, and Hanuman.  ...
